title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. verlotiorum
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. verlotiorum, also known as Wormwood, is a subspecies of the common mugwort plant, characterized by its tall stature and distinctive bitter flavor. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiparasitic agent. The health benefits of Wormwood include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and repelling parasites, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Wormwood is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, and can also be found in some traditional liqueurs like absinthe.
